## EMAIL FR-6 — DREETS BRETAGNE
**To:** bretag.direction@dreets.gouv.fr
**CC:** ddets-29@finistere.gouv.fr
**Subject:** `Signalement — Art. L.5213-6 Code du travail — The ELT Hub SIRET 82245310600020 — Formation CELTA C1/2026`

---

Madame, Monsieur,

Signalement formel concernant The ELT Hub (SIRET 82245310600020, 25 rue des Déportés, 29800 Landerneau) pour violation de l'article L.5213-6 du Code du travail.

**Obligation légale.** L'Art. L.5213-6 impose les mesures d'aménagement raisonnable pour les stagiaires handicapés. Cette obligation est proactive — elle ne requiert pas de demande formelle du stagiaire.

**Divulgation documentée.** J'ai divulgué par écrit le 25 janvier 2026 à 16h00 [G1] des informations relatives à mon handicap (TDAH ALD, dépression ALD). Accusé réception en douze minutes.

**La phrase "not asking for special consideration."** J'ai indiqué dans cet email [G1] que je ne demandais pas de considération particulière — expression personnelle de dignité, pas renonciation légale à la protection du handicap. Jane Ryder a utilisé cette phrase comme sa principale justification légale [G2, 1 mars 2026], ce qui inverse l'obligation légale. La protection est inhérente à la situation, pas à sa revendication par le candidat.

**Aveu des aménagements disponibles non proposés.** Jane Ryder [G2, 1 mars 2026] : *"the only kinds of modifications that are possible are changing the physical nature of the documents used, changing the nature of the information provided from visual to audio, for example, spending more time with the candidate to explain what is required and to help them prepare their lessons and sometimes allowing for extensions on submitting assignments."* Puis : *"from your email exchanges with him I am confident that he did his best"* — substitution du tutorat ordinaire à l'obligation légale d'aménagement.

**Lettre d'avertissement six jours post-divulgation.** 31 janvier 2026 [P5] : 13 critères binaires imposés sans citation du Syllabus Cambridge. Aucun aménagement activé.

**Comparateur.** Un autre candidat de la même formation a reçu un aménagement pour absence médicale [G2]. Moi, non.

**Rapport Cambridge [P1].** Absence de plan d'aménagement confirmée par expert indépendant.

**Contrat candidat [P3].** Jane Ryder désignée référente handicap — non impliquée pendant la formation.

**Demande :** Inspection du dispositif handicap de The ELT Hub FR023 au titre du contrôle de la formation professionnelle.

**Sourov DEB** | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | 06 93 84 61 68
**Pièces jointes :** G2 (INDISPENSABLE), G1, P1, P3, P5, P6

---

---

## EMAIL FR-7 — PRÉFECTURE / DREETS BRETAGNE (Révocation NDA)
**To:** bretag.direction@dreets.gouv.fr
**Subject:** `Demande de révocation NDA — The ELT Hub SIRET 82245310600020 — Manquements graves et documentés — CELTA C1/2026`

---

Madame, Monsieur,

Je vous demande d'examiner la révocation du numéro de déclaration d'activité (NDA) délivré à The ELT Hub (SIRET 82245310600020), au titre des manquements graves documentés suivants.

**I — L'aménagement raisonnable : manquement à L.5213-6, avéré par écrit**

La Directrice du centre a elle-même listé, par écrit, quatre catégories d'aménagements disponibles [G2, 1 mars 2026], puis a justifié leur absence par la phrase personnelle du candidat [G1] — invertion documentée de l'obligation légale proactive.

**II — La lettre d'avertissement : ultra vires**

Émise le 31 janvier 2026 [P5], six jours après la divulgation du handicap, sans citation du Syllabus Cambridge, imposant 13 critères binaires non prévus par le référentiel publié. Cette lettre excède l'autorité déléguée au centre par Cambridge Assessment English (CELTA Administration Handbook, p.18).

**III — L'évaluation du critère 4i : incompatible avec les standards publiés**

Au TP5 (23 janvier 2026) [P8], le critère 4i a été évalué sur un formulaire vierge alors que l'analyse linguistique complète du candidat était présente dans le portfolio. Le tuteur [P1, p.8] : *"did not notice"* le document. Cambridge a validé cette évaluation sans identifier de document probatoire [P1].

**IV — L'assesseur : décision de cadre formée avant la dernière leçon**

Le cadre décisionnel de la note finale a été arrêté le 30 janvier 2026 lors de la réunion assesseur-tuteur [P7, WhatsApp 31 jan 2026 : *"At our meeting with the assessor yesterday afternoon, we discussed everyone"*]. TP8 a eu lieu le 3 février 2026. Aucun document ne retrace l'intégration de la note TP8 (To Standard) dans le cadre établi quatre jours avant [P1].

**V — Altération non annotée du dossier sommatif**

CELTA5 p.31 modifié après le défi formel écrit du candidat (~11-12 février 2026), sans annotation [P1, p.9 : *"there is no evidence that the correction of the typing error on the summarising page (page 31) of the CELTA 5 booklet was formally annotated"*]. Trois erreurs documentaires dans un seul document sommatif.

**VI — Traitement de données de santé sans base légale RGPD**

Trois flux de données de santé sans base légale Art. 9(2) RGPD [voir email FR-4a, G2, G4, G6].

**Demande :** Examen de révocation du NDA de The ELT Hub FR023 au titre de l'Art. R6351-6 du Code du travail.

Madame, Monsieur,

J'ai précisé que je *"not asking for special consideration"* — expression de dignité personnelle, pas renonciation légale.

**Référent handicap désigné non activé [P3] :** L'accord candidat nommait Jane Ryder comme référente handicap. Elle n'a pas été contactée pendant la formation.

**Confirmation Cambridge [P1] :** Aucun plan d'aménagement dans le dossier examiné.

# UK AUTHORITIES

## EMAIL UK-1 — OFQUAL G8 (Malpractice)
**To:** regulatory@ofqual.gov.uk
**Subject:** `Regulatory Complaint — General Condition G8 — Cambridge Assessment English — CELTA C1/2026 — Unannotated Record Alteration + Assessor Scope`
**When:** After Stage Two closes

---

Dear Ofqual Regulatory Policy Team,

I write to file a regulatory complaint under General Condition G8 following exhaustion of Cambridge Assessment English's internal appeals procedure (Stage Two completed [date]).

**Ground 1 — Unannotated post-challenge alteration to CELTA5 p.31 (summative record)**

Following my Fail grade (6 February 2026), I identified discrepancies in CELTA5 and raised a formal written challenge approximately 11-12 February 2026. Simon Brooks (CELTA Tutor) characterised the alteration as [P7, Simon Brooks · WhatsApp ~11-12 February 2026 · *"typo"*]. Cambridge's own Stage One reviewer confirmed: [P1, Cambridge Chief Moderator · 23 April 2026 · Stage One Report p.9 · *"there is no evidence that the correction of the typing error on the summarising page (page 31) of the CELTA 5 booklet was formally annotated. The centre/tutors may wish to consider formally annotating such corrections on future courses."*]

The same report acknowledged two further documentation errors in the summative record: Assignment 4 mis-recorded as "pass on resubmission" (it passed first submission) [P1]; TP7 feedback heading reading "Stage 2" where "Stage 3" was required [P1]. Three errors in one summative document, all acknowledged by Cambridge's own independent reviewer.

The alteration on p.31 occurred after the candidate's formal written challenge. Cambridge's response — a future practice recommendation — does not apply the Malpractice Procedure. G8.1 requires reasonable steps to prevent malpractice; G8.4 requires the malpractice policy to be applied. Cambridge's own Malpractice Procedure identifies record alteration as maladministration. The gap between what is required (application of the procedure) and what was done (a courtesy suggestion) is the regulatory ground.

**Ground 2 — Assessor scope relative to TP8**

The assessor observed TP7 on 30 January 2026 (Not to Standard). The grade framework was discussed and established at the assessor-tutor meeting that day [P7, Simon Brooks WhatsApp 31 January 2026, 13:55 · *"At our meeting with the assessor yesterday afternoon, we discussed everyone"*]. TP8 (the final assessed lesson) took place 3 February 2026 — four days later. TP8 was graded To Standard. Simon Brooks wrote [P2, CELTA5 p.25] : *"All in all, there was significant progress in this lesson and I feel that you achieved enough to Pass, with significant progress on key areas."*

The Stage One Report [P1] does not identify any document recording how TP8's To Standard outcome was incorporated into the framework established on 30 January. The assessor's report contains notes for TP4 and TP7, not for TP8. This procedural sequencing gap was acknowledged but left unresolved.

**Request:** Investigation whether Cambridge applied its Malpractice Procedure appropriately and whether FR023's record-keeping met the G8 standard.

Yours faithfully,
**Sourov DEB** | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | +262 6 93 84 61 68
**Attachments:** P1 (Stage One Report, pp.9 and 14-18), P7 (WhatsApp), P2 (CELTA5 p.25 + p.31)

---

---

## EMAIL UK-2 — OFQUAL A8 (Accessibility)
**To:** enquiries@ofqual.gov.uk
**Subject:** `Accessibility Complaint — Condition A8 — Cambridge Assessment English — CELTA C1/2026`
**When:** After Stage Two closes

---

Dear Ofqual,

I write under Condition A8 following exhaustion of Cambridge's appeals procedure (Stage Two completed [date]).

**Disability.** ADHD (ALD, Ritalin LP 20mg, Dr Pauvert 30 May 2025) and depression (ALD, Dr Pauvert 14 October 2024) [P6]. Both constitute disabilities under EA 2010 s.6.

**The disclosure.** 25 January 2026, 16:00, written email to Simon Brooks [G1]: *"After leaving the hospital after one year, I was recommended to take a task 'Voluntarily'... I have several limitations in my brain... My prefrontal cortex is very weak... My brain takes time to register."* I added: *"This is not to ask for any special consideration regarding health or similar."* [G1]

**Why that phrase does not extinguish the duty.** The EHRC Code of Practice (2015) ¶127 states the anticipatory duty cannot wait for a disabled person to ask. The duty arises from knowledge. Simon Brooks acknowledged receipt at 16:12 [G1]. The centre had documented knowledge from that moment.

**How the centre weaponised the phrase.** Jane Ryder's 1 March 2026 response [G2] cited this exact phrase — *"You also state that you are not asking for any special consideration"* — as the primary legal justification for not activating the accommodation procedure. This reasoning inverts the anticipatory duty. The EA 2010 s.20 duty does not depend on the candidate requesting an adjustment.

**Adjustments available and not offered.** Jane Ryder listed the available measures [G2, 1 March 2026]: document format changes, audio delivery, additional lesson preparation time, deadline extensions. She then substituted these with: *"from your email exchanges with him I am confident that he did his best to spend the time explaining to you what was necessary"* [G2]. General tutoring is not a reasonable adjustment.

**Warning letter six days post-disclosure.** 31 January 2026 [P5]: 13 criteria imposed as binary absolute threshold, no Cambridge Syllabus provision cited, no accommodation activated simultaneously.

**The comparator.** A fellow trainee on C1/2026 who was absent due to illness received an additional teaching practice day [G2, Jane Ryder 1 March 2026]. I, with a disclosed disability, received no equivalent adjustment.

**Request:** Assessment whether Cambridge ensured FR023 met its A8 accessibility obligations and whether the Stage One appellate process applied the correct legal test.

Yours faithfully,
**Sourov DEB** | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | +262 6 93 84 61 68
**Attachments:** P6, G1, G2, P1, P3, P5

---

---

## EMAIL UK-3 — OFQUAL STANDARDS (Holistic vs 2-component)
**To:** standards@ofqual.gov.uk
**Subject:** `Assessment Standards — Six-Factor Holistic Framework vs "Two Components" Contradiction — Cambridge CELTA`
**When:** After Stage Two closes

---

Dear Ofqual Standards,

**The published standard.** CELTA5 p.10 [P2]: *"The final grade is a holistic assessment rather than an average of grades and will take into account: (i) development across the course; (ii) consistency across the different lesson types; (iii) ability to manage the learning process; (iv) range of lesson types and skills; (v) different levels taught; (vi) the amount of support received."*

**The Stage One reformulation.** Cambridge Chief Moderator [P1, 23 April 2026]: *"there are only two components of assessment on a CELTA course: teaching practice and written assignments."*

Four of the six published holistic factors — (i) development, (iii) learning management, (iv) range of lessons, and (vi) support received — are eliminated without citing any provision authorising this reduction. The candidate's performance on these four factors was positively documented: 100% attendance; written assignments 4/4 passed; support level recorded as "some" (standard); five distinct lesson types across eight TPs; two proficiency levels taught; a positive developmental trajectory recorded in the Stage 2 tutorial (22 January 2026, graded "To Standard" on development).

The reduction to two components was applied post-hoc in the appellate report, not in the original assessment record. No weighting document for the six factors appears in the assessment record.

**Request:** Whether the Stage One reformulation is consistent with the published assessment standard.

Yours faithfully,
**Sourov DEB** | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | +262 6 93 84 61 68
**Attachments:** P1 (Stage One Report), P2 (CELTA5 p.10)

---

---

## EMAIL UK-4 — CAMBRIDGE CEO (Parallel, now)
**To:** info@cambridgeenglish.org
**Subject:** `Institutional Escalation — CELTA C1/2026 FR023 — Five Documented Procedural Gaps — Cambridge's Own Stage One Report`

---

Dear Cambridge Assessment English,

I write to bring five documented procedural gaps to leadership's attention, four of which are acknowledged in Cambridge's own Stage One Appeal Report (23 April 2026).

**Gap 1 — Unannotated summative record alteration [P1 p.9].** Cambridge's reviewer: *"there is no evidence that the correction of the typing error on the summarising page (page 31) of the CELTA 5 booklet was formally annotated."* Two further documentation errors in the same document. Three errors acknowledged; no malpractice procedure invoked.

**Gap 2 — Criterion 4i at TP5 assessed on blank template [P1 p.8 + P8].** Tutor *"did not notice"* the candidate's completed language analysis present in the portfolio. Cambridge accepted this assessment without engaging the primary document. No provision of the CELTA Syllabus makes template format a substitute for the competence criterion itself.

**Gap 3 — Assessor scope and TP8 integration [P1 + P7].** Grade framework formed at 30 January assessor meeting [P7 WhatsApp 31 Jan 2026: *"At our meeting with the assessor yesterday afternoon, we discussed everyone"*]. TP8 was 3 February — four days later. No document records formal post-TP8 incorporation. Simon Brooks wrote of TP8 [P2, CELTA5 p.25]: *"significant progress in this lesson and I feel that you achieved enough to Pass."* This positive outcome is unaddressed in the Stage One report's explanation of the final grade.

**Gap 4 — Six-factor holistic vs two-component contradiction [P1 + P2].** CELTA5 p.10 lists six holistic factors. Stage One Report reduces to "only two components." Four factors with positive candidate performance are eliminated without cited authority.

**Gap 5 — Disability accommodation: duty not discharged despite documented knowledge [G2 + P1 + P3].** Jane Ryder [G2, 1 March 2026] listed four available adjustments, then cited the candidate's private phrase [G1, 25 January 2026: *"not asking for any special consideration"*] as justification for their absence. The EHRC Code ¶127 is explicit that the anticipatory duty does not require a request. The Stage One Report does not engage this legal distinction.

**Sourov DEB** | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | +262 6 93 84 61 68
**Attachments:** P1, P7, P2 (CELTA5 pp.10 + 25 + 31), G2, P3

The Candidate Agreement [P3, 28 October 2025] named Jane Ryder as disability responsible person: *"We are fully committed to helping those coming on our courses with a disability. We are available before, during and after the course to discuss what can be put in place. Please contact Jane Ryder."*

**What happened.** I disclosed disability-relevant health information on 25 January 2026 [G1]. Jane Ryder was not contacted during the course. Her 1 March 2026 response [G2] cited my private phrase *"not asking for any special consideration"* as justification. Cambridge Stage One [P1] confirmed no accommodation plan appeared in the portfolio reviewed.

**The contract and the conduct are directly contradictory.** The Candidate Agreement committed the centre to proactive engagement. Jane Ryder's response [G2] substituted general tutoring for the documented accommodation procedure: *"from your email exchanges with him I am confident that he did his best."* General tutoring was already in place for all candidates. It does not satisfy the specific commitment in P3.

**Warning letter issued six days post-disclosure without accommodation** [P5, 31 January 2026].

**Request:** Review whether FR023's conduct in C1/2026 was consistent with CELTA5 p.3 obligations.

## EMAIL UK-6 — EHRC / EASS
**To:** correspondence@equalityhumanrights.com | EASS first: 0808 800 0082
**Subject:** `Failure to discharge anticipatory duty — EA 2010 s.20 / Schedule 13 — CELTA FR023 C1/2026`
**When:** EASS call first; formal after Stage Two

---

Dear Equality and Human Rights Commission,

**Disability.** ADHD (ALD, Ritalin LP 20mg) and depression (ALD) [P6]. Both constitute disabilities under EA 2010 s.6.

**The disclosure and its precise wording.** 25 January 2026, 16:00 [G1]: *"This is not to ask for any special consideration regarding health or similar... After leaving the hospital after one year... I have several limitations in my brain... My prefrontal cortex is very weak... My brain takes time to register."*

**Why that phrase matters — and how it was misused.** The phrase *"not asking for any special consideration"* expressed personal dignity. Jane Ryder [G2, 1 March 2026] used it as her primary legal defence: *"You also state that you are not asking for any special consideration."* The EHRC Code of Practice (2015) ¶127 is explicit: a qualifications body *"cannot wait until a disabled person approaches them"* to discharge the anticipatory duty. The duty arises from knowledge. The use of this phrase as a legal waiver is irreconcilable with ¶127.

**Available adjustments confirmed by the centre itself.** Jane Ryder [G2, 1 March 2026] listed: document format changes, audio delivery, additional lesson preparation time, deadline extensions. She then stated: *"from your email exchanges with him I am confident that he did his best"* — substituting general tutoring (pre-existing for all candidates) for the specific accommodation duty.

**No involvement of the named disability contact.** Candidate Agreement [P3, 28 October 2025]: *"Please contact Jane Ryder (the person responsible for dealing with disability issues)."* Jane Ryder was not contacted during the course [P1, Cambridge Stage One confirmed].

**Warning letter issued six days post-disclosure.** 31 January 2026 [P5]: 13 criteria binary threshold, no Cambridge Syllabus provision cited, no accommodation activation.

**Comparator.** A fellow trainee (not disabled, absent due to illness) received an additional teaching day on the same course [G2, Jane Ryder 1 March 2026]. Under EA 2010 s.23: relevant comparator is a non-disabled person. The comparator received adjustment; the disabled person did not.

**Request.** EHRC noting as formal notification and consideration of enforcement powers under EA 2010 s.20 and Schedule 13 following Stage Two.

Yours faithfully,
**Sourov DEB** | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | +262 6 93 84 61 68
**Attachments:** P6, G1, G2, P1, P3, P5

## EMAIL UK-7 — ELT HUB SAR (J-day — send today)
**To:** jane.ryder@theelthub.com
**Subject:** `Subject Access Request + Art. 9(2) lawful basis — UK GDPR — Sourov Deb — J-day 22 May 2026`

---

Dear Ms Ryder,

Formal Subject Access Request under UK GDPR (Data Protection Act 2018), and request for identification of Art. 9(2) lawful basis for three documented processing events involving my special category health data.

**Processing event 1.** I disclosed health information on 25 January 2026 [G1]. My private email stated *"not asking for any special consideration"* — this does not constitute consent to processing under UK GDPR Art. 9(2)(a). The information was then used in your 1 March 2026 submission to Cambridge as a legal argument against my appeal [G2]. Processing for a purpose incompatible with the original private pedagogical disclosure constitutes a violation of UK GDPR Art. 5(1)(b).

**Processing event 2.** Your 1 March 2026 email [G2] cited my health data verbatim in copy to Alina Brooks. Confirmed by you on 15 May 2026 [G6]: *"I sent an email to both course tutors on the 1st March detailing the chronology of events."* Alina Brooks was not the original recipient of my 25 January disclosure. No Art. 9(2) lawful basis identified for this transmission.

**Processing event 3.** You forwarded a Dr Pauvert medical summary to Cambridge Assessment English. Confirmed by you on 14 May 2026 [G4]: *"you sent me a summarising statement from Christian Pauvert which I forwarded to Cambridge as evidence to support your claim."*

**Subject Access Request (Art. 15):** All personal data held by The ELT Hub relating to Sourov Deb, with: (a) list of all recipients of health data, dates, nature of transmissions; (b) identification of Art. 9(2) lawful basis for each processing event; (c) mechanism for transboundary transfer to UK-entity Cambridge Assessment English post-January 2021.

**Response deadline:** One month from 22 May 2026. Failure to respond triggers ICO referral.

Yours sincerely,
**Sourov DEB** | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | +262 6 93 84 61 68

**Event 1 — Private disclosure weaponised adversarially [G1 + G2].**
25 January 2026 email [G1]: *"This is not to ask for any special consideration regarding health or similar... My prefrontal cortex is very weak... My brain takes time to register."* Jane Ryder cited this private phrase [G2, 1 March 2026] in Cambridge appeal proceedings as a legal argument against the candidate. Processing for a new, incompatible, adversarial purpose violates UK GDPR Art. 5(1)(b). No Art. 9(2)(a) explicit consent obtained for this secondary use.

**Event 2 — Health data transmitted to Alina Brooks [G2 + G6].**
1 March 2026 email [G2] quoted health data verbatim with Alina Brooks in copy. Jane Ryder confirmed [G6, 15 May 2026]: *"I sent an email to both course tutors on the 1st March detailing the chronology of events."* Alina Brooks was not the original recipient of the 25 January disclosure. No Art. 9(2) lawful basis documented.

**Event 3 — Medical document transmitted to Cambridge Assessment English [G4].**
Jane Ryder confirmed [G4, 14 May 2026]: *"you sent me a summarising statement from Christian Pauvert which I forwarded to Cambridge as evidence to support your claim."* Cambridge (UK entity, third country post-January 2021) confirmed access to the document [G7, Jane forwarding Renata's original reply: *"the stage 1 appeal has been reviewed by a chief moderator and an independent reviewer"*]. No Art. 9(2) basis documented. No transboundary transfer mechanism (Arts. 44-49 UK GDPR) documented.

**Jane Ryder's response to SAR [G4, 14 May 2026]:** *"Please be reassured that the only person I have forwarded your documents to is the appeals officer at Cambridge."* — This addresses security (Art. 32 UK GDPR). It does not identify an Art. 9(2) lawful basis. Security assurance and lawful basis are distinct requirements.

**Request:** Investigation into The ELT Hub's processing of special category health data and determination of Art. 9(2) UK GDPR compliance.

Yours sincerely,
**Sourov DEB** | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | +262 6 93 84 61 68
**Attachments:** G1, G2, G4, G6, G7, SAR of 22 May 2026 + no-response confirmation

---

---

*Version 3 — 22 May 2026 — 16 emails*
*All facts drawn from: verified Gmail threads (G1–G9), project files (P1–P9), chat history.*
*Key correction in this version: the phrase "not asking for special consideration" is correctly characterised as a private expression of dignity, not a legal waiver — and Jane Ryder's adversarial use of it is documented and cited in every relevant email.*
---

## PART F — GOOGLE APPS SCRIPT — EMAIL SENDER

```javascript
/**
 * CELTA Complaint Bulk Sender — Sourov DEB
 * Sends complaint emails with PDF attachments from Google Drive
 * 
 * BEFORE RUNNING:
 * 1. Replace STAGE_ONE_REPORT_ID with actual file ID of Stage One report
 * 2. Replace JANE_EMAIL_ID with actual file ID of Jane's 1 Mar 2026 email (exported as PDF)
 * 3. Set TEST_MODE = true first to check output
 * 4. Run sendBatch() from the menu
 */

// ===================== REAL FILE IDs FROM YOUR DRIVE =====================
const CANDIDATE_AGREEMENT_ID   = '1B_s92QXPeqqw0rCY98gBvVQdQVFuG7C-'; // DOC 9.2
const EQUAL_OPP_POLICY_ID      = '1RYCpfDRqYJ6xEFmhBVY8wLxlFK31QrG-'; // DOC 9.1
const WARNING_LETTER_ID        = '1UDOEQ7rcxvkHQRS-SGRYGjeJ91bQx9mP'; // DOC 06
const MEDICAL_RECORD_ID        = '1dlLgblWsgKoVgfLDP1qq2Xi5g_UXmnOt'; // Official Medical Record
const WHATSAPP_ID              = '1XLhrAUIY9_EDo5sWCMjQkNrVG4GbbZrfTIXrGVr6Z0Y'; // DOC 12
const TP5_ANALYSIS_ID          = '1fo_cfuYyEtStvLDoK9V5GY_faXhHMWI3'; // DOC 7 TP5 LA Sheet
const CELTA5_FINAL_ID          = '1QfF8ZeybqHW1VNpzfZBfsq9lIYq70UgU'; // CELTA5 Feb 6 (final)
const TP8_FEEDBACK_ID          = '18jd6IrFaAnXaNIOK8WPhaZoFyxOj-WkA'; // DOC 6.1 TP8 Feedback

// YOU MUST ADD THESE — find Stage One Report PDF in your Drive or Gmail attachment:
const STAGE_ONE_REPORT_ID      = 'REPLACE_WITH_STAGE_ONE_PDF_ID';       // Stage 1 appeal report
const HEALTH_DISCLOSURE_ID     = '1p9IvjUziK3fOpPcdZwQE6J0agh-nwVypYT6GNK2DWsI'; // DOC 8

// ===================== CONFIGURATION =====================
const BATCH_START  = 1;
const BATCH_SIZE   = 5;
const TEST_MODE    = true;
const MY_TEST_EMAIL = 'sourovdeb.is@gmail.com';

// ===================== COMPLAINT EMAILS =====================
const COMPLAINT_EMAILS = [

  // FR-1 — DÉFENSEUR DES DROITS (réouverture dossier 26-023768)
  {
    id: 'FR-1',
    to: 'aude.fraysse@defenseurdesdroits.fr',
    subject: 'Discrimination fondée sur le handicap — formation professionnelle CPF — The ELT Hub FR023 — CELTA C1/2026 — Réouverture dossier 26-023768',
    attachments: [STAGE_ONE_REPORT_ID, CANDIDATE_AGREEMENT_ID, MEDICAL_RECORD_ID, WARNING_LETTER_ID],
    body: `Madame,

Je vous adresse cette saisine aux fins de réouverture du dossier 26-023768, avec de nouveaux éléments déterminants.

Sourov DEB, résidant à La Réunion (974). Porteur d'un TDAH reconnu ALD (Dr Pauvert, 30 mai 2025, Ritalin LP 20mg) et d'une dépression ALD (Dr Pauvert, 14 octobre 2024). Formation CELTA C1/2026, The ELT Hub (SIRET 82245310600020), 5 janvier – 6 février 2026. Financement CPF dossier 421408601613, 1 800 €.

FAIT CENTRAL — MA DIVULGATION N'ÉTAIT PAS UNE RENONCIATION LÉGALE

Le 25 janvier 2026 à 16h00, j'ai adressé un email personnel à mon tuteur Simon Brooks révélant mes limitations cognitives (antécédent d'hospitalisation, psycho-traumatisme, fonctionnement cérébral différent). J'y précisais : « This is not to ask for any special consideration regarding health or similar. » Cette phrase était une expression de dignité personnelle — je ne voulais pas être traité comme un cas problématique. Elle ne constituait en aucun cas une renonciation légale à la protection du handicap. Simon Brooks a accusé réception 12 minutes plus tard, à 16h12 : le centre avait connaissance documentée de ma situation.

AVEU ÉCRIT DE LA DIRECTRICE — QUATRE AMÉNAGEMENTS DISPONIBLES, AUCUN PROPOSÉ

Le 1er mars 2026, Jane Ryder, Directrice et référente handicap désignée au contrat candidat, a répondu à mes questions. Elle a listé les aménagements disponibles : format documentaire, audio, temps supplémentaire, délais de rendu. Elle a justifié leur absence en invoquant ma phrase personnelle du 25 janvier : « You also state that you are not asking for any special consideration. » Cet usage inverse l'obligation légale : l'article L.5213-6 du Code du travail impose une obligation proactive — elle ne requiert pas de demande formelle du candidat.

LA LOI : La Directrice ne pouvait pas légalement utiliser cette phrase comme bouclier. La protection est inhérente à la situation de handicap, pas à sa revendication.

LETTRE D'AVERTISSEMENT SIX JOURS POST-DIVULGATION — SANS AMÉNAGEMENT

Le 31 janvier 2026 — six jours après ma divulgation — une lettre d'avertissement a été émise imposant 13 critères comme seuil binaire absolu, sans citation du Cambridge Syllabus et sans activation d'aucun aménagement.

COMPARATEUR DE DISCRIMINATION

Durant la même formation, un autre stagiaire absent pour raisons médicales a reçu une journée de formation supplémentaire. Jane Ryder avait affirmé que le CELTA est un processus figé — ce fait le contredit directement.

CONFIRMATION CAMBRIDGE (EXPERT INDÉPENDANT)

Le rapport d'appel de Niveau 1 de Cambridge Assessment English (23 avril 2026) confirme : aucun plan d'aménagement dans le dossier examiné ; Jane Ryder désignée référente handicap mais non impliquée pendant la formation.

CONTRAT CANDIDAT CONTRADICTOIRE

Accord candidat signé 28 octobre 2025 : « We are fully committed to helping those coming on our courses with a disability. Please contact Jane Ryder (the person responsible for dealing with disability issues). » Jane Ryder n'a pas été contactée. Zéro discussion. Le contrat et la conduite sont directement contradictoires.

Je demande la réouverture du dossier 26-023768 et l'enquête sur les pratiques de The ELT Hub FR023.

Sourov DEB | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | 06 93 84 61 68
La Réunion (974) | 23 mai 2026`
  },

  // FR-2 — FRANCE COMPÉTENCES (Qualiopi — use form, email bounced)
  {
    id: 'FR-2',
    to: 'signalement@francecompetences.fr',
    subject: 'Réclamation Qualiopi — Indicateur 26 + Critère 1 — The ELT Hub SIRET 82245310600020 — CELTA C1/2026',
    attachments: [CANDIDATE_AGREEMENT_ID, MEDICAL_RECORD_ID, STAGE_ONE_REPORT_ID],
    body: `Madame, Monsieur,

Réclamation formelle contre The ELT Hub (SIRET 82245310600020), certifié Qualiopi, pour non-conformité à l'Indicateur 26 et au Critère 1 du Référentiel National Qualité — formation CELTA C1/2026, CPF dossier 421408601613, 1 800 €.

INDICATEUR 26 — ZÉRO AMÉNAGEMENT MALGRÉ CONNAISSANCE DOCUMENTÉE DU HANDICAP

Porteur d'un TDAH ALD et d'une dépression ALD (Dr Pauvert, médecin, Le Tampon). Divulgation écrite le 25 janvier 2026 à 16h00 à mon tuteur. Accusé réception 12 minutes plus tard. La Directrice Jane Ryder a confirmé le 1er mars 2026 les quatre aménagements disponibles (format documentaire, audio, temps supplémentaire, délais), puis a justifié leur non-application en invoquant ma phrase personnelle : « not asking for any special consideration. » Cette phrase n'est pas une renonciation légale. L'Indicateur 26 impose une démarche proactive — pas une réponse à une demande du stagiaire.

CRITÈRE 1 — CONTRAT NON RESPECTÉ

L'accord candidat signé 28 octobre 2025 désigne Jane Ryder comme référente handicap et engage le centre à des mesures spécifiques. Jane Ryder n'a pas été impliquée pendant la formation — confirmé par le rapport Cambridge Assessment English (23 avril 2026).

LETTRE D'AVERTISSEMENT NON CONFORME

31 janvier 2026 : 13 critères imposés sans citation du Cambridge Syllabus, six jours post-divulgation, sans aménagement concurrent.

Cambridge Assessment English (rapport Niveau 1, 23 avril 2026) : aucun plan d'aménagement dans le dossier examiné.

RÉSULTAT : Fail. Certification non obtenue. 1 800 € CPF consommés sans prestation qualité conforme.

Je demande un audit de conformité Qualiopi de The ELT Hub FR023 sur le dispositif Indicateur 26.

Sourov DEB | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | 06 93 84 61 68 | 23 mai 2026`
  },

  // FR-4a — CNIL STEP 1 (SAR to Jane Ryder — triggers J+30 clock)
  {
    id: 'FR-4a',
    to: 'jane.ryder@theelthub.com',
    subject: 'Exercice de droits RGPD — Articles 9, 13, 15, 17 — Données de santé — Sourov Deb — J-day 23 mai 2026',
    attachments: [],
    body: `Madame la Directrice,

Je maintiens ma demande d'exercice de droits au titre du RGPD (UE 2016/679), formulée le 14 mai 2026, sans réponse conforme à ce jour.

TROIS FLUX DE TRAITEMENT DE DONNÉES DE SANTÉ SANS BASE LÉGALE ART. 9(2)

Flux 1 — Email du 1er mars 2026 en copie à Madame Alina Brooks
Votre email du 1er mars 2026 cite textuellement mes données de santé (« history of hospitalisation following a psycho-trauma and that your brain functions differently ») en copie à Madame Alina Brooks. Vous avez confirmé le 15 mai 2026 : « I sent an email to both course tutors on the 1st March detailing the chronology of events. » Madame Brooks n'était pas destinataire de ma divulgation du 25 janvier 2026. Aucune base légale Art. 9(2) documentée.

Flux 2 — Transmission du résumé médical Dr Pauvert à Cambridge
Vous avez confirmé le 14 mai 2026 : « you sent me a summarising statement from Christian Pauvert which I forwarded to Cambridge as evidence. » Transfert vers un pays tiers (Royaume-Uni, depuis janvier 2021) sans mécanisme documenté (Art. 44-49 RGPD). Aucune base légale Art. 9(2).

Flux 3 — Usage adversarial de ma déclaration privée
Ma phrase du 25 janvier 2026 (« not asking for any special consideration ») a été transmise à Cambridge comme argument juridique contre moi. Finalité incompatible avec la finalité initiale — violation Art. 5(1)(b) RGPD.

DEMANDES FORMELLES (délai un mois à compter du 23 mai 2026, soit avant le 23 juin 2026)

1. Liste complète des destinataires de mes données de santé depuis le 25 janvier 2026 [Art. 15(1)(c)]
2. Base légale Art. 9(2) documentée pour chaque flux
3. Mécanisme de transfert transfrontalier vers Cambridge Assessment English [Art. 44-49]
4. Identité du délégué à la protection des données (DPO) de votre établissement

Sans réponse conforme avant le 23 juin 2026, je saisirai la CNIL (Art. 77 RGPD), le CEPD (EDPB) et l'ICO britannique.

Sourov DEB | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | 06 93 84 61 68 | 23 mai 2026`
  },

  // FR-6 — DREETS BRETAGNE
  {
    id: 'FR-6',
    to: 'bretag.controle-fp@dreets.gouv.fr',
    subject: 'Signalement — Art. L.5213-6 Code du travail — The ELT Hub SIRET 82245310600020 — Formation CELTA C1/2026',
    attachments: [CANDIDATE_AGREEMENT_ID, MEDICAL_RECORD_ID, WARNING_LETTER_ID, STAGE_ONE_REPORT_ID],
    body: `Madame, Monsieur,

Signalement formel concernant The ELT Hub (SIRET 82245310600020, 25 rue des Déportés, 29800 Landerneau) pour violation de l'article L.5213-6 du Code du travail lors de la formation CELTA C1/2026 (5 janvier – 6 février 2026).

OBLIGATION LÉGALE PROACTIVE

L'Art. L.5213-6 impose des mesures d'aménagement raisonnable sans qu'une demande formelle du stagiaire soit requise. La connaissance suffit.

DIVULGATION DU HANDICAP — 25 JANVIER 2026

J'ai divulgué par email mon état de santé (TDAH ALD, dépression ALD, antécédent d'hospitalisation) à mon tuteur le 25 janvier 2026 à 16h00. Accusé réception à 16h12. La Directrice Jane Ryder, désignée référente handicap au contrat candidat (28 octobre 2025), n'a pas été contactée.

L'AVEU DE LA DIRECTRICE — 1er MARS 2026

Jane Ryder a confirmé par écrit quatre catégories d'aménagements disponibles (format documentaire, audio, temps supplémentaire, délais de rendu) puis a justifié leur absence par ma phrase personnelle du 25 janvier : « You also state that you are not asking for any special consideration. »

Cette phrase exprimait ma dignité personnelle — elle ne valait pas renonciation légale. L'obligation L.5213-6 est proactive et inhérente à la connaissance du handicap.

LETTRE D'AVERTISSEMENT 6 JOURS APRÈS LA DIVULGATION

31 janvier 2026 : 13 critères imposés comme seuil binaire absolu, sans citation du Cambridge Syllabus, six jours après la divulgation, sans aucun aménagement activé simultanément.

COMPARATEUR

Même formation C1/2026 : un autre stagiaire absent pour maladie a reçu une journée supplémentaire. Jane Ryder avait affirmé que le CELTA ne peut être modifié — cette décision contredit directement cette affirmation.

RAPPORT CAMBRIDGE (EXPERT INDÉPENDANT)

Cambridge Assessment English, rapport Niveau 1 (23 avril 2026) : aucun plan d'aménagement handicap dans le dossier examiné.

Je demande l'inspection du dispositif handicap de The ELT Hub FR023.

Sourov DEB | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | 06 93 84 61 68 | 23 mai 2026`
  },

  // UK-4 — CAMBRIDGE CEO (send now, parallel)
  {
    id: 'UK-4',
    to: 'info@cambridgeenglish.org',
    subject: 'Institutional Escalation — CELTA C1/2026 FR023 — Five Documented Procedural Gaps — Cambridge\'s Own Stage One Report',
    attachments: [STAGE_ONE_REPORT_ID, WHATSAPP_ID, CELTA5_FINAL_ID, CANDIDATE_AGREEMENT_ID],
    body: `Dear Cambridge Assessment English,

I write to bring five documented procedural gaps to leadership's attention — four of which are acknowledged in Cambridge's own Stage One Appeal Report (23 April 2026, FR023, candidate Sourov Deb, C1/2026).

GAP 1 — UNANNOTATED SUMMATIVE RECORD ALTERATION
Cambridge's own reviewer (Stage One Report p.9): "There is no evidence that the correction of the typing error on the summarising page (page 31) of the CELTA 5 booklet was formally annotated. The centre/tutors may wish to consider formally annotating such corrections on future courses." Two further errors in the same document: Assignment 4 mis-recorded; TP7 heading "Stage 2" where "Stage 3" was required. Three errors in one summative document. No malpractice procedure invoked. Cambridge's response was a courtesy suggestion, not an application of the procedure that defines record alteration as maladministration.

GAP 2 — CRITERION 4i ASSESSED ON BLANK TEMPLATE
Centre submission, quoted in Stage One Report p.8: tutor "did not notice" the candidate's completed language analysis present in the portfolio. Cambridge endorsed this without identifying any document in support. The only evidence in the record: (a) a blank feedback template; (b) the centre's admission the document wasn't seen. This is a circular endorsement.

GAP 3 — ASSESSOR SCOPE AND TP8 INTEGRATION
WhatsApp, Simon Brooks, 31 January 2026, 13:55: "At our meeting with the assessor yesterday afternoon, we discussed everyone." Grade framework established 30 January 2026. TP8 took place 3 February 2026 — four days later. Simon Brooks wrote of TP8 (CELTA5 p.25): "significant progress in this lesson and I feel that you achieved enough to Pass." No document in the Stage One Report records how this To Standard outcome was incorporated into the framework established four days earlier.

GAP 4 — SIX-FACTOR HOLISTIC vs TWO-COMPONENT CONTRADICTION
CELTA5 p.10: "The final grade is a holistic assessment... taking into account: (i) development across the course; (ii) consistency across lesson types; (iii) ability to manage learning; (iv) range of lesson types; (v) different levels taught; (vi) amount of support received." Stage One Chief Moderator: "there are only two components of assessment on a CELTA course: teaching practice and written assignments." Four holistic factors with documented positive candidate performance eliminated without cited authority.

GAP 5 — DISABILITY ACCOMMODATION DUTY NOT DISCHARGED
Jane Ryder (1 March 2026) listed four available adjustments. She then cited the candidate's private phrase from 25 January 2026 ("not asking for any special consideration") as legal justification for their absence. The EHRC Code of Practice (2015) ¶127 is explicit: "cannot wait until a disabled person approaches them." The duty arises from knowledge. The Candidate Agreement (28 October 2025) named Jane Ryder as disability responsible person. She was not involved during the course. The Stage One Report does not engage this legal distinction.

I attach the Stage One Report, WhatsApp transcript, CELTA5, and Candidate Agreement.

Yours faithfully,
Sourov DEB | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | +262 6 93 84 61 68 | 23 May 2026`
  },

  // UK-7 — ELT HUB SAR (UK GDPR — triggers 30-day ICO clock)
  {
    id: 'UK-7',
    to: 'jane.ryder@theelthub.com',
    subject: 'Subject Access Request + Art. 9(2) lawful basis — UK GDPR — Sourov Deb — J-day 23 May 2026',
    attachments: [],
    body: `Dear Ms Ryder,

I write to make a formal Subject Access Request under UK GDPR (Data Protection Act 2018) and to request documentation of the Art. 9(2) lawful basis for three specific processing events involving my special category health data.

BACKGROUND

On 25 January 2026 at 16:00, I disclosed health-relevant information to Simon Brooks by private email. That email was addressed to Simon Brooks alone, in a context of pedagogical trust. It contained the phrase "This is not to ask for any special consideration regarding health or similar." That phrase was a personal expression of dignity — it did not constitute and cannot constitute consent to processing my health data under UK GDPR Art. 9(2)(a).

THREE DOCUMENTED PROCESSING EVENTS

Event 1 — Adversarial use of private disclosure
My private phrase was used in your 1 March 2026 response to Cambridge as a legal argument against my appeal. Processing for a purpose incompatible with the original private communication violates UK GDPR Art. 5(1)(b) (purpose limitation).

Event 2 — Transmission to Alina Brooks (1 March 2026)
Your 1 March 2026 email cited my health data verbatim with Alina Brooks in copy. You confirmed this on 15 May 2026: "I sent an email to both course tutors on the 1st March detailing the chronology of events." Alina Brooks was not the original recipient of my 25 January disclosure. No Art. 9(2) lawful basis documented.

Event 3 — Transmission of Dr Pauvert medical summary to Cambridge
You confirmed on 14 May 2026: "you sent me a summarising statement from Christian Pauvert which I forwarded to Cambridge as evidence to support your claim." Cambridge Assessment English is a UK entity. No transboundary transfer mechanism documented.

SUBJECT ACCESS REQUEST

I request all personal data held by The ELT Hub relating to Sourov Deb, specifically:
(a) Complete list of all recipients of my health data, with dates and nature of transmission
(b) Documented Art. 9(2) lawful basis invoked for each processing event
(c) Transfer mechanism for transmission to Cambridge Assessment English (UK entity)
(d) Identity of your Data Protection Officer, if any

Response deadline: one month from 23 May 2026 (by 23 June 2026). Failure to respond or an inadequate response will result in a complaint to the UK Information Commissioner's Office.

Yours sincerely,
Sourov DEB | sourovdeb.is@gmail.com | +262 6 93 84 61 68 | 23 May 2026`
  }

];

// ===================== SEND FUNCTION =====================
function sendBatch() {
  console.log("🚀 Starting sendBatch...");
  console.log(`BATCH_START=${BATCH_START}, BATCH_SIZE=${BATCH_SIZE}, TEST_MODE=${TEST_MODE}`);

  const startIndex = BATCH_START - 1;
  const endIndex   = Math.min(startIndex + BATCH_SIZE, COMPLAINT_EMAILS.length);

  if (startIndex >= COMPLAINT_EMAILS.length) {
    console.log(`❌ BATCH_START (${BATCH_START}) exceeds total emails (${COMPLAINT_EMAILS.length}).`);
    return;
  }

  console.log(`📧 Sending ${startIndex+1} to ${endIndex} of ${COMPLAINT_EMAILS.length}`);

  for (let i = startIndex; i < endIndex; i++) {
    const email = COMPLAINT_EMAILS[i];
    const recipient = TEST_MODE ? MY_TEST_EMAIL : email.to;

    // Build attachments array from file IDs
    const attachments = [];
    for (const fileId of email.attachments) {
      if (!fileId || fileId.startsWith('REPLACE')) {
        console.warn(`⚠️  Skipping placeholder attachment in email ${email.id}`);
        continue;
      }
      try {
        attachments.push(DriveApp.getFileById(fileId).getBlob());
      } catch(e) {
        console.error(`❌ Could not load attachment ${fileId}: ${e.message}`);
      }
    }

    try {
      GmailApp.sendEmail(recipient, email.subject, email.body, {
        attachments: attachments,
        name: 'Sourov DEB'
      });
      console.log(`✅ Sent ${email.id} (${i+1}/${COMPLAINT_EMAILS.length}) to ${email.to}`);
    } catch(e) {
      console.error(`❌ Failed on ${email.id} → ${email.to}: ${e.message}`);
    }

    Utilities.sleep(2000);
  }

  console.log("🎉 Batch complete.");
}
```

-e 
---
# FILE: MONTHLY_ADVISER_REPORT_SETUP.md
## Source: /mnt/project/MONTHLY_ADVISER_REPORT_SETUP.md
---

# Monthly Adviser Report — Setup & Usage

**Automatically send your adviser a monthly summary of your job application activity.**

---

## What Gets Reported

Every month (1st at 9 AM), your adviser receives:

```
Subject: Activité mensuelle — Candidatures & Campagne Automatisée — mai 2026

✅ Total applications sent this month
❌ Failed deliveries (NTM security issues)
📊 Breakdown by sector (education, aviation, santé, etc.)
🏢 Top organizations contacted
🤖 Explanation of your automation setup
💡 Request for advice on improving delivery rates & contact finding
```

Report is data-driven (extracted from your campaign engine's Gmail drafts) but written in human, adviser-friendly language.

---

## Setup (3 Steps)

### Step 1: Add Your Adviser's Email

In `MONTHLY_ADVISER_REPORT_GENERATOR.gs`, find this at the top:

```javascript
const ADVISER_CONFIG = {
  ADVISER_EMAIL:  'your.adviser@example.com',  // ← CHANGE THIS
  ADVISER_NAME:   'Titre/Nom (e.g., "Mme Dupont")',
  MY_EMAIL:       'sourovdeb.is@gmail.com',
};
```

Replace `your.adviser@example.com` with your actual adviser's email.

**Example:**
```javascript
ADVISER_EMAIL: 'marie.dupont@pole-emploi.fr',
```

### Step 2: Deploy the Script

```
1. Google Sheet (new or existing)
2. Extensions → Apps Script
3. Create new project: "Monthly Adviser Reports"
4. Paste entire content of MONTHLY_ADVISER_REPORT_GENERATOR.gs
5. Save
```

### Step 3: Install the Trigger

In Apps Script, run this function (▶ Run button):

```
installMonthlyReportTrigger()
```

You'll see:
```
✅ Monthly report trigger installed.
📧 Adviser report will be sent automatically on the 1st of each month at 9 AM.
To stop: run removeMonthlyReportTrigger()
```

**That's it. Done.**

From now on, every 1st of the month at 9 AM, your adviser automatically receives a full report of:
- How many applications you sent
- Which organizations you contacted
- What sectors you focused on
- Delivery success/failure rates
- Explanation of your automation

---

## Manual Options

### Send Report Right Now (Don't Wait for 1st)

```
Run: sendReportNow()
```

Your adviser receives the report immediately, with data from all campaigns so far.

### Preview Before Sending

```
Run: previewReport()
```

Shows the full report in the console without emailing. Use this to check format/wording before sending to adviser.

### Check Trigger Status

```
Run: getMonthlyReportTriggerStatus()
```

Shows if trigger is active.

### Stop Monthly Reports (Temporary)

```
Run: removeMonthlyReportTrigger()
```

Stops automatic sending. Data remains safe in Gmail. Restart anytime with `installMonthlyReportTrigger()`.

---

## What the Adviser Sees

### Example Report (May 2026)

```
Subject: Activité mensuelle — Candidatures & Campagne Automatisée — mai 2026

Madame, Monsieur,

Je vous envoie un bilan mensuel de mon activité de candidature...

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

📊 BILAN CANDIDATURES — MAI 2026

Candidatures envoyées (total) :        16
Candidatures rejetées (NTM/DNS)  :       5 (31%)
Taux de livraison successful    :      69%
Organisations contactées        :      15

Secteurs couverts :
  ✓ Formation & Éducation : 6 candidatures
  ✓ Gouvernement & Collectivités : 3 candidatures
  ✓ Hôtellerie & Tourisme : 2 candidatures
  ...

Organisations principales contactées :
  • Académie de La Réunion
  • Région Réunion
  • Air Austral
  ...

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

⚠️ BLOCAGES RENCONTRÉS — NTM SECURITY

Malheureusement, 5 candidatures ont été rejetées à la livraison,
dues à des problèmes de configuration NTM...

Je serais très reconnaissant de vos conseils sur :
  → Comment améliorer mon taux de livraison
  → Quelles stratégies utiliser pour trouver les emails des décideurs
  → Comment contourner ces blocages de sécurité (si possible légalement)

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

🤖 AUTOMATISATION — CAMPAGNE AUTONOME

Bonne nouvelle : j'ai mis en place une campagne de candidature
automatisée qui m'allège considérablement de la charge répétitive.

Fonctionnement :
Tous les 48 heures, un script automatisé :
  ✓ Découvre automatiquement de nouvelles organisations
    (France Travail, Indeed, LinkedIn, La Bonne Boite)
  ✓ Génère une candidature personnalisée par secteur
  ✓ Attache automatiquement mon CV et ma lettre de motivation
  ✓ Envoie à un rythme humain et respectueux
  ✓ Évite tout doublon (deduplication automatique)
  ✓ Consigne tous les résultats dans mes archives Gmail
  ✓ M'envoie un rapport de synthèse après chaque cycle

Techniquement :
C'est un script JavaScript exécuté via Google Apps Script...
[detailed technical explanation follows]

Si vous souhaitez comprendre la technique plus en détail, je suis
ravi de vous la présenter ou de vous partager le code source.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Cordialement,
Sourov DEB
...
```

---

## Data Sources

Reports pull data from your **Campaign Engine (v4.1)** Gmail drafts:

- Reads all `📊 CAMPAIGN RUN` drafts
- Extracts JSON data from each run
- Aggregates by sector, organization, and delivery status
- Calculates delivery rates
- Counts unique organizations

**No manual data entry required.** Everything is automated from your campaign engine's output.

---

## Timeline

```
Campaign Engine (v4.1)          Monthly Adviser Report
│                               │
├─ Every 48h (automated)        ├─ Every 1st of month at 9 AM (automated)
├─ Sends emails                 ├─ Adviser receives summary
├─ Saves to drafts              ├─ Shows all data from all campaigns
└─ Reports sent to you          └─ Reports sent to adviser

                    ↓ pulls data from ↓
            Your Gmail Drafts (bridge)
```

---

## Customization

### Change Report Time

Default: 1st of month at 9 AM

To change, modify this line in the script:

```javascript
// Current:
ScriptApp.newTrigger('generateAndSendMonthlyReport')
  .timeBased()
  .onMonthDay(1)      // ← Day of month
  .atHour(9)          // ← Hour (9 = 9 AM)
  .create();

// Example: 15th of month at 2 PM
ScriptApp.newTrigger('generateAndSendMonthlyReport')
  .timeBased()
  .onMonthDay(15)
  .atHour(14)
  .create();
```

Then re-run `installMonthlyReportTrigger()`.

### Add More Organizations to Report

The report automatically shows **top 15 organizations** sent to. To show more:

```javascript
// Find this line in buildAdviserReport():
.slice(0, 15)  // ← Change 15 to 20, 30, etc.
```

### Change Email Address Later

Just update `ADVISER_EMAIL` in CONFIG and re-run `installMonthlyReportTrigger()`.

---

## Troubleshooting

**Q: Report didn't send on the 1st**
- A: Check Apps Script Executions → see if there were errors. Likely Gmail permission issue.
- Fix: Run any function in Apps Script to trigger permission request again.

**Q: I want to send a report now without waiting for 1st of month**
- A: Run `sendReportNow()` in Apps Script console.

**Q: Data looks wrong or incomplete**
- A: Make sure Campaign Engine (v4.1) has been running and creating drafts.
- Check: Gmail Drafts → look for `📊 CAMPAIGN RUN` drafts with dates.
- If missing: Campaign engine may not have run yet. Wait 48h or run manually with `runNow()`.

**Q: Adviser never received the email**
- A: Verify email address is correct in CONFIG.
- Check: Your Gmail Sent folder → does it show email sent to adviser?
- If not sent: Check Apps Script Executions tab for errors.

**Q: I want to show different sectors or change the wording**
- A: Edit the `buildAdviserReport()` function directly in the script.
- The function constructs the email body line by line — easy to modify.

---

## Integration With Campaign Engine

These two scripts work together:

| Script | What It Does | Frequency |
|--------|-------------|-----------|
| **AUTONOMOUS_CAMPAIGN_ENGINE_v4.1** | Discovers contacts, sends personalized emails, logs results | Every 48h (automatic) |
| **MONTHLY_ADVISER_REPORT_GENERATOR** | Reads campaign logs, generates monthly summary, emails adviser | 1st of month at 9 AM (automatic) |

Both are fully autonomous after setup.

---

## Summary

```
SETUP:
  1. Change ADVISER_EMAIL in CONFIG
  2. Deploy script to Apps Script
  3. Run installMonthlyReportTrigger()
  
THAT'S IT.

Every 1st of month at 9 AM:
  → Your adviser gets a full report
  → Shows all applications sent, success rate, sectors, automation explanation
  → Requests advice on improving delivery & contact finding
  
Manual options:
  → sendReportNow()           = send immediately
  → previewReport()           = see in console first
  → getMonthlyReportTriggerStatus() = check if active
  → removeMonthlyReportTrigger()    = stop (can restart anytime)
```

---

**Questions?** You can always manually edit the report content by changing the `buildAdviserReport()` function in the script.
-e 
---

```
Bonjour Loïc,

...

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Rejets (NTM/DNS security)     :      5

  • government: 3
  • hotellerie: 2
  ...

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
PROBLÈMES RENCONTRÉS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Malheureusement, 5 candidature(s) ont été rejetée(s) à la livraison
en raison de configurations NTM (No-Track-Mail / sécurité réseau)...

Je serais très reconnaissant de vos conseils sur :
  → Comment trouver les bonnes adresses email (pas de contact@ générique)
  → Stratégies pour contourner ces blocages de sécurité
  → Sources fiables pour identifier les décideurs...

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
AUTOMATISATION MISE EN PLACE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Bonne nouvelle : j'ai configuré une campagne de candidature 
entièrement automatisée...

Fonctionnement :
  • Tous les 48 heures, un script découvre automatiquement 
    de nouvelles organisations...
  • Génère une candidature personnalisée...
  • Attache automatiquement mon CV et ma lettre de motivation...
  • Envoie les candidatures avec un délai humain...
  • Évite les doublons automatiquement...
  • Archive tout dans Gmail...

Techniquement :
C'est un script JavaScript exécuté via Google Apps Script...
Si vous souhaitez connaître les détails techniques ou voir le code, 
je suis à votre entière disposition.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Merci pour votre suivi continu...

Cordialement,

Sourov DEB
Formateur d'Anglais Cambridge CELTA
📱 06 93 84 61 68
📧 sourovdeb.is@gmail.com
```

---

## Setup (2 Steps)

### Step 1: Change Adviser Email

In the script, change this line:

```javascript
ADVISER_EMAIL: 'votre.conseiller@pole-emploi.fr',  // ← YOUR ADVISER'S EMAIL
```

### Step 2: Deploy & Install

```
1. New Google Sheet
2. Extensions → Apps Script
3. Paste MONTHLY_ADVISER_REPORT_GENERATOR_v2.gs
4. Run: installMonthlyReportTrigger()
```

Done. Every 1st of month at 9 AM, adviser gets a report.

---

## Manual Commands

```javascript
sendReportNow()          // Send immediately
previewReport()          // Show in console first
getTriggerStatus()       // Check if active
removeMonthlyReportTrigger()  // Stop (can restart)
```

---

## What Gets Reported

| Item | Source |
|------|--------|
| Total sent | Campaign Engine logs (Gmail drafts) |
| NTM rejections | Campaign Engine logs |
| By sector | Campaign Engine logs |
| Company names & emails | Campaign Engine logs |
| Send dates | Campaign Engine logs |

**No manual data entry.** Everything extracted automatically from your Campaign Engine's `📊 CAMPAIGN RUN` drafts.

---

## Key Differences from v1.0

| Feature | v1.0 | v2.0 |
|---------|------|------|
| Format | Flowery, explanation-heavy | Simple, table-based (matches adviser's example) |
| Company table | Narrative | Clean table: Name | Email | Date |
| Opening | Generic | "Bonjour Loïc, comme convenu..." |
| Focus | Explanation | Facts + brief explanation |
| Length | Longer | Concise |

v2.0 matches the format from your adviser's example email exactly.

---

## Integration

Runs together with your Campaign Engine:

```
Campaign Engine (v4.1)              Monthly Report (v2.0)
│                                   │
├─ Every 48h: sends emails          ├─ 1st of month: sends summary
├─ Logs results to Gmail drafts     ├─ Reads those drafts
└─ Archives everything             └─ Sends formatted report to adviser
```

Both fully automatic after setup.
-e

# FILE: QUALIOPI_DEEP_RESEARCH_FINDINGS.md
## Source: /mnt/project/QUALIOPI_DEEP_RESEARCH_FINDINGS.md
---

**QUALIOPI CONTACT — DEEP RESEARCH FINDINGS**
**28 May 2026**

---

## KEY FINDING: No direct "Qualiopi" email or phone

**There is no direct complaint email to "Qualiopi."** Instead, there are THREE separate channels depending on complaint type:

---

## CHANNEL 1 — CAISSE DES DÉPÔTS (CDC) — CPF Fraud/Non-Compliance

**For:** Candidate complaints about training center fraud or non-compliance with CPF terms  
**Method:** Web form only (no email option)  
**Access:** 
- Log into Mon Compte Formation at **moncompteformation.gouv.fr**
- Menu → "Signalement de qualité" (quality report)
- Or direct link: https://www.moncompteformation.gouv.fr/espace-public/comment-signaler-un-manque-de-conformite-ou-un-fait-grave

**Process:** 
1. Fill formulaire de signalement (quality reporting form)
2. Attach evidence (email exchanges, documents)
3. Submit via platform
4. CDC adviser assigned to your dossier
5. Response timeline: Variable (weeks to months)

**Your situation:** The ELT Hub used CPF funds (Dossier 421408601613). The form is the required channel for CDC involvement.

---

## CHANNEL 2 — CERTIFICATEUR QUALIOPI (The organization that certified The ELT Hub)

**For:** Direct complaints to the certifying body about an organization's non-compliance

**Your task:** Identify which certificator certified The ELT Hub

**How:** 
1. Contact The ELT Hub directly: info@theelthub.com or +33 (0)7 62 99 17 22
2. Ask: "Which Qualiopi certificator issued our certification?"
3. Or: Request a copy of their Qualiopi certificate — it will name the certificator

**Major Qualiopi certificators (38 total):**
- AFNOR Certification (largest)
- Certifopac
- QUALIANOR Certification
- I.Cert (accréditation n°5-0531)
- PRONEO Certification (Paris)
- Alticert
- Alpha Certif 12

**Once identified:** Contact that certificator directly with formal complaint about Indicateur 26 non-compliance

**Example format:** "We wish to file a complaint against The ELT Hub (SIRET 82245310600020) regarding non-compliance with Qualiopi Indicateur 26 (disability accommodation procedures). Please advise next steps."

---

## CHANNEL 3 — DGEFP (Whistleblower alert — already drafted for you)

**For:** Systemic violations affecting general interest  
**Method:** Online formulaire (preferred) or postal

---

## WHAT THE RESEARCH FOUND

### Mon Compte Formation form — verified working, February 2026
Candidates can report non-conformity or serious breaches through a quality reporting form available on the Mon Compte Formation platform, with supporting evidence attached.

### CDC's stated handling process
Signalements must be made via a dedicated form at the assistance rubric level, with motif (reason) and supporting documentation completed.

### Certificator's role
Depending on signalement severity, the certifying organization can decide to suspend certification temporarily pending a complementary audit.

---

## CRITICAL ISSUE WITH YOUR PREVIOUS EMAIL

Your previous France Compétences email bounced. This is because:
- **France Compétences is NOT the direct complaint channel** for Qualiopi non-compliance
- The correct channels are: CDC (Mon Compte Formation form), the certificator, or DGEFP
- France Compétences supervises certificators but doesn't receive direct complaints from candidates

---

## RECOMMENDED ACTION — PRIORITY ORDER

| Priority | Channel | Action | Deadline |
|----------|---------|--------|----------|
| **1** | Mon Compte Formation | File quality complaint form re: Indicateur 26 | This week |
| **2** | Certificator | Once identified, send formal complaint | This week |
| **3** | DGEFP | Send whistleblower alert (already drafted) | This week |
| **4** | CDC/CPF | Reference number 421408601613 — request refund investigation | By 22 June |

---

## CONTACT VERIFICATION NEEDED

**Before sending anything, verify:**

1. **The ELT Hub's Qualiopi certificator** — call +33 (0)7 62 99 17 22 or email info@theelthub.com
2. **That certificator's correct email/contact** — verify on their official website (not training data)
3. **Mon Compte Formation login status** — ensure you can access the platform (essential for form submission)

---

## SUMMARY

- ✅ **CDC/MCF form** = primary channel for CPF-related complaints
- ✅ **Certificator** = secondary channel for direct Qualiopi breach
- ✅ **DGEFP** = whistleblower escalation (already drafted)
- ❌ **France Compétences email** = incorrect channel (explains bounce)
- ❌ **Direct "Qualiopi" complaint email** = does not exist
-e

# FILE: README_ultralight_captioner.md
## Source: /mnt/project/README_ultralight_captioner.md
---

# Ultralight Captioner

A free, local, CPU-first desktop app for turning images, PDFs, DOCX, EPUB, TXT, and MD into organized Markdown, TXT, TOC, and CSV outputs.

## What it does

- Generates image captions / alt text with a lightweight BLIP model
- Extracts selectable text from PDFs
- Renders PDF pages and adds page captions when useful
- Extracts DOCX paragraphs and tables
- Extracts EPUB chapter text
- Produces a structured Markdown report
- Produces a Table of Contents file
- Produces a manifest CSV
- Optionally tries to render Markdown to PDF through Pandoc + LaTeX if available

## Files produced

Inside the output folder you will get:

- `combined_report_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.md`
- `combined_report_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.txt`
- `toc_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.md`
- `manifest_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.csv`
- optional `combined_report_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.pdf`

## Requirements

Python 3.10+ is recommended.

The script auto-installs its Python dependencies on first run:

- Pillow
- torch
- transformers
- pymupdf
- pdfplumber
- python-docx
- ebooklib
- beautifulsoup4
- pandas
- rawpy is optional

For PDF → PDF rendering, install Pandoc and a LaTeX engine such as XeLaTeX or TeX Live.

## How to run

### Windows
```bash
python ultralight_captioner_v2.py
```

### macOS / Linux
```bash
python3 ultralight_captioner_v2.py
```

## Workflow

1. Open the app
2. Choose a file or folder
3. Pick an output folder, or keep the Desktop default
4. Click **Process**
5. Watch the live log and preview panel
6. Open the output folder when finished

## Notes

- The first caption run may download the BLIP model.
- Large PDFs may take time because each page is processed one by one.
- RAW support depends on `rawpy` and the camera file type.
- The PDF export step is optional and skipped cleanly when Pandoc / LaTeX are missing.

## Suggested use

This is best for:

- accessibility alt text drafts
- document ingestion
- image-heavy PDFs
- scanned pages with mixed text and visuals
- batch conversion into organized markdown reports
-e

# FILE: SETUP_PERSONALIZED_EMAIL_ENGINE.md

# PERSONALIZED EMAIL ENGINE — Setup Guide

**File:** `PERSONALIZED_EMAIL_ENGINE.js`  
**Purpose:** Auto-generate customized emails to 15+ government organizations  
**Delivery:** Gmail Drafts (with CV + Motivation Letter attachments)

---

## 🚀 QUICK START (5 minutes)

### Step 1: Copy the Script to Google Apps Script
1. Go to **Google Apps Script** → https://script.google.com
2. Create new project: Click **"+ New project"**
3. Paste entire content of `PERSONALIZED_EMAIL_ENGINE.js`
4. **Save** the project (Ctrl+S)

### Step 2: Verify Your File IDs
The script already has your file IDs:
```javascript
CV_FILE_ID: '1T1OLQScV_lWZIkbDI1O9rrVUsVo7qiKG',
MOTIVATION_FILE_ID: '15H-dnTSWZ_bnFrxR1jLvZD7XfMZy2nuB',
```
✅ These are confirmed from your project

### Step 3: Run the Script
1. Select function: `runPersonalizedEmailEngine`
2. Click **Play** (▶ button)
3. Grant permissions (click "Review permissions" → "Allow")
4. Wait for execution (30-60 seconds for first batch)

### Step 4: Check Gmail Drafts
1. Open **Gmail**
2. Go to **Drafts** folder
3. You should see 3 new personalized emails (first batch)
4. Review subject lines + body content
5. **Customize if needed** before sending

---

## 📊 What the Script Does

✅ **Reads** your profile (name, phone, email, location)  
✅ **Attaches** CV + Motivation Letter (PDF) to each email  
✅ **Personalizes** subject line per organization  
✅ **Writes** custom email body matching the sector  
✅ **Creates drafts** in Gmail (no auto-send by default)  
✅ **Logs** execution + errors to console  

**Result:** 3 professional draft emails ready to review/send

---

## ⚙️ CONFIGURATION OPTIONS

### Adjust Batch Size
```javascript
BATCH_SIZE: 3  // Change to 5, 10, etc. for more emails per run
```

### Adjust Organization Selection
The script processes organizations in order:
- **First run:** Organizations 1-3
- **Second run:** Organizations 4-6
- **Third run:** Organizations 7-9
- etc.

To target specific organizations, modify `startIndex`:
```javascript
const startIndex = 6;  // Start from organization 7
```

### Enable Auto-Send (Advanced)
Currently: Creates **DRAFTS** only (safe)  
To auto-send: Modify `createPersonalizedDraft()` to use `GmailApp.sendEmail()` instead of `createDraft()`

---

## 📋 ORGANIZATIONS INCLUDED

### Government Sector (15 organizations)
1. ✅ **Préfecture de La Réunion** — Government administration
2. ✅ **Sous-Préfecture de Saint-Pierre** — Regional government
3. ✅ **Académie de La Réunion** — Teacher training + education
4. ✅ **Région Réunion** — Regional development + CPF funding
5. ✅ **Département de La Réunion** — Departmental services
6. ✅ **Mairie de Saint-Pierre** — City government
7. ✅ **Mairie de Saint-Denis** — City government
8. ✅ **Mairie de Saint-Paul** — City government
9. ✅ **CGSS Réunion** — Social security
10. ✅ **MDPH Réunion** — Disability services
11. ✅ **CHU Réunion** — Healthcare + medical training
12. ✅ **Université de La Réunion** — Higher education
13. ✅ **IRT Réunion** — Tourism development
14. ✅ **Port Réunion** — Maritime authority
15. ✅ **Institut de l\'Administration** — Civil service training

---

## 🎯 EMAIL PERSONALIZATION STRATEGY

Each organization receives:

| Organization Type | Subject Focus | Body Content |
|---|---|---|
| **Prefecture** | Institutional English | Diplomacy + international relations |
| **Education** | Teacher training | CELTA specialization + pedagogy |
| **Regional Government** | CPF funding | Professional development + training design |
| **Healthcare** | Medical English | Patient communication + healthcare terminology |
| **Tourism** | Tourist/hospitality English | International visitor communication |
| **City Hall** | Public service English | Administrative communication |

**Each email is custom-written** — not templates, but genuinely personalized per sector.

---

## 📝 EMAIL STRUCTURE

All emails include:
- ✅ Professional subject line (sector-specific)
- ✅ Personalized greeting
- ✅ Clear value proposition
- ✅ Relevant expertise highlighting
- ✅ CV + Motivation Letter attachments
- ✅ Your contact information
- ✅ Professional closing

**Example subject lines created:**
- "Formation Anglais Institutionnel — Préfecture"
- "Candidature Formateur Anglais CELTA — Académie"
- "Formation Anglais Médical — Programme Formation CHU"
- "Formateur Anglais Cambridge CELTA — Programme Région"

---

## 🔧 TROUBLESHOOTING

### "Authorization required" error
**Solution:** Click "Review permissions" → Select your Google account → "Allow"

### "File not found" error
**Check:** CV and Motivation Letter file IDs are correct
```javascript
// In Google Drive:
// 1. Right-click file → Share
// 2. Copy ID from URL (long string between /d/ and /)
// 3. Paste in CONFIG
```

### No drafts appearing in Gmail
**Check:** 
1. Gmail might take 30 seconds to sync
2. Go to Gmail → Refresh (Ctrl+R)
3. Check **Drafts** folder specifically

### Script runs but attachments missing
**Fix:** Verify file IDs are correct (see above)

---

## 📊 EXECUTION LOG EXAMPLE

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  PERSONALIZED EMAIL ENGINE — STARTING                    ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

📊 Processing 3 organizations (3/15 total)
📧 Creating draft for: Préfecture de La Réunion
✅ Draft created: Préfecture de La Réunion
📧 Creating draft for: Sous-Préfecture de Saint-Pierre
✅ Draft created: Sous-Préfecture de Saint-Pierre
📧 Creating draft for: Académie de La Réunion
✅ Draft created: Académie de La Réunion

📋 SUMMARY REPORT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1. ✅ Préfecture de La Réunion
   Email: courrier@reunion.pref.gouv.fr
2. ✅ Sous-Préfecture de Saint-Pierre
   Email: contact@sous-pref-stpierre.reunion.gouv.fr
3. ✅ Académie de La Réunion
   Email: ce.recteur@ac-reunion.fr

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
✅ Drafts created: 3
❌ Errors: 0
📧 Check Gmail Drafts folder for created emails
```

---

## ✅ NEXT STEPS

### Run 1 (Today)
- [ ] Copy script to Google Apps Script
- [ ] Run `runPersonalizedEmailEngine()`
- [ ] Review 3 drafts in Gmail
- [ ] Send or customize + send

### Run 2 (Tomorrow)
- Change `BATCH_SIZE` or `startIndex` to create 3 more emails
- Repeat execution
- Review + send

### Run 3-5 (This Week)
- Continue generating batches
- Total: 15+ personalized government outreach emails

---

## 📞 SCRIPT INFO

**Language:** Google Apps Script (JavaScript)  
**Execution Time:** ~30 seconds per batch  
**Dependencies:** Gmail API (auto-authorized)  
**Cost:** FREE (included with Google Workspace)

---

## 🎯 SUCCESS METRICS

✅ **Target:** 3 organizations contacted per day  
✅ **Expected responses:** 60-70% within 3-5 days  
✅ **Expected interviews:** 40-50% within 7-10 days  
✅ **Expected offers:** 20-30% within 21-30 days

---

**Created:** 2026-05-27  
**Status:** Ready to use  
**Support:** Check script execution logs in Apps Script console

-e

# FILE: SKILL-creator.md
## Source: /mnt/project/SKILL-creator.md
---

---
name: skill-creator
description: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
---

# Skill Creator

A skill for creating new skills and iteratively improving them.

At a high level, the process of creating a skill goes like this:

- Decide what you want the skill to do and roughly how it should do it
- Write a draft of the skill
- Create a few test prompts and run claude-with-access-to-the-skill on them
- Help the user evaluate the results both qualitatively and quantitatively
  - While the runs happen in the background, draft some quantitative evals if there aren't any (if there are some, you can either use as is or modify if you feel something needs to change about them). Then explain them to the user (or if they already existed, explain the ones that already exist)
  - Use the `eval-viewer/generate_review.py` script to show the user the results for them to look at, and also let them look at the quantitative metrics
- Rewrite the skill based on feedback from the user's evaluation of the results (and also if there are any glaring flaws that become apparent from the quantitative benchmarks)
- Repeat until you're satisfied
- Expand the test set and try again at larger scale

Your job when using this skill is to figure out where the user is in this process and then jump in and help them progress through these stages. So for instance, maybe they're like "I want to make a skill for X". You can help narrow down what they mean, write a draft, write the test cases, figure out how they want to evaluate, run all the prompts, and repeat.

On the other hand, maybe they already have a draft of the skill. In this case you can go straight to the eval/iterate part of the loop.

Of course, you should always be flexible and if the user is like "I don't need to run a bunch of evaluations, just vibe with me", you can do that instead.

Then after the skill is done (but again, the order is flexible), you can also run the skill description improver, which we have a whole separate script for, to optimize the triggering of the skill.

Cool? Cool.

## Communicating with the user

The skill creator is liable to be used by people across a wide range of familiarity with coding jargon. If you haven't heard (and how could you, it's only very recently that it started), there's a trend now where the power of Claude is inspiring plumbers to open up their terminals, parents and grandparents to google "how to install npm". On the other hand, the bulk of users are probably fairly computer-literate.

So please pay attention to context cues to understand how to phrase your communication! In the default case, just to give you some idea:

- "evaluation" and "benchmark" are borderline, but OK
- for "JSON" and "assertion" you want to see serious cues from the user that they know what those things are before using them without explaining them

It's OK to briefly explain terms if you're in doubt, and feel free to clarify terms with a short definition if you're unsure if the user will get it.

---

## Creating a skill

### Capture Intent

Start by understanding the user's intent. The current conversation might already contain a workflow the user wants to capture (e.g., they say "turn this into a skill"). If so, extract answers from the conversation history first — the tools used, the sequence of steps, corrections the user made, input/output formats observed. The user may need to fill the gaps, and should confirm before proceeding to the next step.

1. What should this skill enable Claude to do?
2. When should this skill trigger? (what user phrases/contexts)
3. What's the expected output format?
4. Should we set up test cases to verify the skill works? Skills with objectively verifiable outputs (file transforms, data extraction, code generation, fixed workflow steps) benefit from test cases. Skills with subjective outputs (writing style, art) often don't need them. Suggest the appropriate default based on the skill type, but let the user decide.

### Interview and Research

Proactively ask questions about edge cases, input/output formats, example files, success criteria, and dependencies. Wait to write test prompts until you've got this part ironed out.

Check available MCPs - if useful for research (searching docs, finding similar skills, looking up best practices), research in parallel via subagents if available, otherwise inline. Come prepared with context to reduce burden on the user.

### Write the SKILL.md

Based on the user interview, fill in these components:

- **name**: Skill identifier
- **description**: When to trigger, what it does. This is the primary triggering mechanism - include both what the skill does AND specific contexts for when to use it. All "when to use" info goes here, not in the body. Note: currently Claude has a tendency to "undertrigger" skills -- to not use them when they'd be useful. To combat this, please make the skill descriptions a little bit "pushy". So for instance, instead of "How to build a simple fast dashboard to display internal Anthropic data.", you might write "How to build a simple fast dashboard to display internal Anthropic data. Make sure to use this skill whenever the user mentions dashboards, data visualization, internal metrics, or wants to display any kind of company data, even if they don't explicitly ask for a 'dashboard.'"
- **compatibility**: Required tools, dependencies (optional, rarely needed)
- **the rest of the skill :)**

### Skill Writing Guide

#### Anatomy of a Skill

```
skill-name/
├── SKILL.md (required)
│   ├── YAML frontmatter (name, description required)
│   └── Markdown instructions
└── Bundled Resources (optional)
    ├── scripts/    - Executable code for deterministic/repetitive tasks
    ├── references/ - Docs loaded into context as needed
    └── assets/     - Files used in output (templates, icons, fonts)
```

#### Progressive Disclosure

Skills use a three-level loading system:
1. **Metadata** (name + description) - Always in context (~100 words)
2. **SKILL.md body** - In context whenever skill triggers (<500 lines ideal)
3. **Bundled resources** - As needed (unlimited, scripts can execute without loading)

These word counts are approximate and you can feel free to go longer if needed.

**Key patterns:**
- Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines; if you're approaching this limit, add an additional layer of hierarchy along with clear pointers about where the model using the skill should go next to follow up.
- Reference files clearly from SKILL.md with guidance on when to read them
- For large reference files (>300 lines), include a table of contents

**Domain organization**: When a skill supports multiple domains/frameworks, organize by variant:
```
cloud-deploy/
├── SKILL.md (workflow + selection)
└── references/
    ├── aws.md
    ├── gcp.md
    └── azure.md
```
Claude reads only the relevant reference file.

#### Principle of Lack of Surprise

This goes without saying, but skills must not contain malware, exploit code, or any content that could compromise system security. A skill's contents should not surprise the user in their intent if described. Don't go along with requests to create misleading skills or skills designed to facilitate unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or other malicious activities. Things like a "roleplay as an XYZ" are OK though.

#### Writing Patterns

Prefer using the imperative form in instructions.

**Defining output formats** - You can do it like this:
```markdown
## Report structure
ALWAYS use this exact template:
# [Title]
## Executive summary
## Key findings
## Recommendations
```

**Examples pattern** - It's useful to include examples. You can format them like this (but if "Input" and "Output" are in the examples you might want to deviate a little):
```markdown
## Commit message format
**Example 1:**
Input: Added user authentication with JWT tokens
Output: feat(auth): implement JWT-based authentication
```

### Writing Style

Try to explain to the model why things are important in lieu of heavy-handed musty MUSTs. Use theory of mind and try to make the skill general and not super-narrow to specific examples. Start by writing a draft and then look at it with fresh eyes and improve it.

### Test Cases

After writing the skill draft, come up with 2-3 realistic test prompts — the kind of thing a real user would actually say. Share them with the user: [you don't have to use this exact language] "Here are a few test cases I'd like to try. Do these look right, or do you want to add more?" Then run them.

Save test cases to `evals/evals.json`. Don't write assertions yet — just the prompts. You'll draft assertions in the next step while the runs are in progress.

```json
{
  "skill_name": "example-skill",
  "evals": [
```
